columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each employee; sourced from the EMPLOYEE_ID primary key in MySQL agentichub.EMPLOYEES.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Corporate email address for the employee.

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Optional contact phone number; stored as STRING to preserve formatting such as dashes and country codes.

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Calendar date on which the employee was hired.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Code identifying the employee's job role; used as a clustering column.

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee base salary with two decimal places of precision.

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission rate as a percentage; applicable to sales-oriented roles only.

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referencing foreign key to employee_id identifying the direct manager; NULL for top-level employees.

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Identifier for the organisational department; leading clustering column to accelerate department-scoped queries.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE); low-cardinality clustering column.

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp in the source system; used as the BigQuery DAY partition column to enable partition pruning on hourly incremental loads.

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the record in the source system; supports incremental merge logic downstream.

cardinality.notes: Multiple employees can report to the same manager; manager_id is a self-referencing FK to employee_id within the same table. Employees at the top of the hierarchy have a NULL manager_id.
